name: Us Community
description_short: US Community Credit Union is a member-owned financial institution serving Nashville and surrounding Tennessee counties with checking, savings, loans, and digital banking services.
description_long:
US Community Credit Union was founded in 1949 by eleven VA Hospital employees and has grown into a established financial cooperative serving Middle Tennessee. Originally chartered as US Courthouse Credit Union, it expanded its field of membership significantly over decades—first to federal employees in Davidson County (1952), then to Nashville metro employees via merger (2004), and eventually to anyone living, working, worshiping, or attending school in Davidson, Rutherford, Wilson, Sumner, and Williamson Counties (as of 2011).

USCCU offers a comprehensive suite of consumer banking products including checking accounts (Six Star, It's On US, and Scholars Checking), savings products, auto loans (advertised rates as low as 5.59% APR), mortgages for home purchases and refinances, credit cards (rates as low as 13.25% APR), and various loan types including personal and home equity options. Members can access services through physical branches in Nashville, Mt. Juliet, and Antioch, plus online and mobile banking platforms, ATM networks, and customer service representatives.

The credit union emphasizes community investment and member empowerment. In 2023 alone, USCCU made 8,667 loans to underserved populations throughout Middle Tennessee and raised $750,000 for Monroe Carrell Jr. Children's Hospital at Vanderbilt. The organization markets itself as locally-based with real human support alongside digital tools, positioning itself as an alternative to traditional banks with a cooperative, member-focused structure.

As a credit union, USCCU operates on the principle of member ownership and profit-sharing rather than shareholder returns. However, service availability is geographically limited to specific Tennessee counties, and rates/terms vary by product and creditworthiness. The institution appears stable with 75+ years of operating history, though specific current asset size and membership numbers are not disclosed on their website.
services: Home equity loans and lines of credit
Community lending programs for underserved populations
pros: Member-owned credit union structure means profits returned to members rather than external shareholders
Competitive auto loan rates starting at 5.59% APR and credit card rates from 13.25% APR
Multiple physical branch locations (Nashville, Mt. Juliet, Antioch) plus drive-thru services
Strong community investment record: 8,667 loans to underserved populations in 2023, $750K raised for children's hospital
Established 75+ year history dating to 1949, first Tennessee credit union to own its own building (1955)
Comprehensive product suite including checking, savings, auto loans, mortgages, credit cards, and personal loans
Digital banking options including mobile app and online banking alongside in-person service
cons: Membership limited to residents, workers, students, or worshippers in specific Tennessee counties (Davidson, Rutherford, Wilson, Sumner, Williamson) — not open to general public nationwide
Limited branch network (3 locations in Nashville area) compared to major national banks
No specific mention of APY rates for savings accounts or competitive CD rates on website
Website lacks transparency on specific loan terms, approval requirements, or detailed product comparison information
No mention of specialized services like identity theft protection, credit monitoring, or comprehensive financial planning tools
best_for: Middle Tennessee residents (Nashville, Mt. Juliet, Antioch, and surrounding counties) seeking alternative to traditional banks
Members prioritizing community-focused banking and local reinvestment over corporate profits
Individuals employed by federal government, Nashville Metro government, or those with community ties to eligible counties
Borrowers seeking competitive auto loan and mortgage rates through a member-owned cooperative structure
diagnosis: US Community Credit Union is best for Tennessee residents in its service area (Davidson, Rutherford, Wilson, Sumner, Williamson Counties) who value local, member-owned banking and community investment over national bank convenience. The main caveat is strict geographic membership restrictions—eligibility requires living, working, worshiping, or attending school in their specific service counties, making it inaccessible to most of the U.S. population.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_description: US Community Credit Union serves Nashville and Tennessee counties with checking, savings, loans, and digital banking.
